page: Amazon Web Services m7a.24xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated physical cores and 96 vCPUs utilizing the x86_64 AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The memory configuration consists of 384.0 GB of DDR5 RAM operating at 5 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across multiple compute-heavy and memory-intensive workloads. The multi-core CPU performance measured via stress-ng and Geekbench falls into the top 10% tier, supported by strong single-core capabilities. Memory bandwidth tests show top-tier read speeds for small cached blocks and strong performance for larger uncached blocks. In software compilation, ray tracing, and image processing, the instance consistently achieves top-tier results. Additionally, database operations and Redis SET operations demonstrate strong to top-tier execution speeds, with Redis reaching 168.46 million operations per second.

For machine learning tasks, the server delivers top-tier prompt processing speeds for small models and top-tier text generation speeds for medium models, alongside strong performance for large models. However, because the instance lacks dedicated GPU hardware and local storage, it relies entirely on CPU-based execution and network-attached storage. This hardware profile represents a balanced resource density, offering high qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require massive parallel CPU processing and high DDR5 memory bandwidth without the premium cost of specialized GPU accelerators.

The m7a.24xlarge is suited for multi-threaded enterprise applications, software compilation pipelines, high-throughput database operations, static web serving, and CPU-based large language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m7a.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 96 dedicated physical cores and 96 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR5 RAM operating at 5 GHz, delivering 4.0 GB of memory per core. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps. Benchmark results place this server in the top tier for multi-core CPU performance, software compilation, and Redis operations, alongside strong memory bandwidth and database performance. It also demonstrates strong to top-tier capabilities in CPU-based LLM inference. Given its high resource density and lack of specialized accelerators, the m7a.24xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy, non-GPU workloads. It is highly suited for enterprise databases, software compilation, static web serving, and multi-threaded application hosting.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and 38 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and 38 Gbps baseline network speed.
tagline: A high-density, dedicated AMD EPYC platform delivering top-tier multi-core compute and DDR5 memory performance for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz
384.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier multi-core CPU, software compilation, and Redis performance
38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with zero local storage
Suited for enterprise databases, web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference
